title: Setaria globulifera
intro: Setaria globulifera, also known as globe millet or globe bristle grass, is a type of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Setaria globulifera include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Setaria globulifera is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Setaria globulifera is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a type of wild grass commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Setaria globulifera is classified as a member of the subfamily Panicoideae. This classification places it within the larger clade of grasses that includes various other species of grasses. As part of the genus Setaria, it shares characteristics with other wild grasses.
